methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Modern Intelligence, a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by John Dulin, has raised $5.12M to date. Valuation analysis relies on two applicable methods triangulated against a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public firms in the Defense & Security sector.

Stage-progression methodology, applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yields $13M. Peer-median methodology examines four same-sector comparables—three Series A firms (Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, Ares Industries at $150M) and one Seed-stage peer (Tenna Systems at $60M)—producing a median valuation of $225M. The peer range spans $60M to $500M. These two methods anchor a triangulated range with a low of $11M, median of $119M, and high of $259M.

The substantial spread reflects stage heterogeneity within the comp set: stage-progression yields a valuation closer to the low end, while peer-median is pulled higher by three Series A valuations. Material uncertainties include whether Modern Intelligence will achieve Series A traction comparable to named peers, and whether earlier-stage positioning (Tenna Systems at $60M) better reflects execution risk. The $119M median is directionally reasonable but material downside risk exists if the company fails to demonstrate Series A-caliber metrics.
